title: Bare Multisig Standardness
description: Bare multisig outputs are consensus-valid, but default relay policy treats larger bare multisig cases as non-standard and prefers script-hash-based multisig in practice. This distinction matters when reasoning about what the network will relay versus what Script can theoretically validate.

resources.title: Bitcoin Developer Guide: Transactions
resources.notes: Canonical explanation of multisig scripts plus the standardness rule that makes larger bare multisig outputs non-standard for default relay.

resources.title: P2MS - Pay To Multisig
resources.notes: Decoding lesson that explicitly contrasts bare multisig with P2SH multisig and calls out the small-participant standardness limit.

resources.title: Mastering Bitcoin
resources.notes: Broad technical reference for script templates, redeem-script indirection, and policy-versus-validity tradeoffs in older transaction patterns.
